output: Implemented batch 5 / T5 by moving preview overlay editing onto the shared interaction contract: `OverlayEditor` now uses pointer-driven preview ownership, phone touch stays inspector-first, preview/background taps route through shared context state, and shell plus preview controls now expose larger touch targets, live interaction-state labels, and reduced-motion-safe transitions. Verified with type-checking and the full `PreviewPersistence` and `RemotionPreview` test modules, then checkpointed progress to `.megaplan/plans/create-a-concrete-mobile-and-20260410-0149/execution_batch_5.json`.

task_updates.executor_notes: Brought preview overlay editing onto the same provider-backed interaction contract without adding a second mobile preview path. `PreviewPanel` now resolves preview background interactions through pointer events, publishes preview interaction state for assistive tech, and passes `deviceClass`, `inputModality`, `interactionMode`, `gestureOwner`, and gesture-owner setters into `OverlayEditor`. `OverlayEditor` now uses pointer events plus preview gesture ownership, claims `preview` only for actual overlay transforms, keeps phone touch inspector-first by skipping direct transform starts and hiding touch-direct manipulation controls there, and enlarges touch-visible preview controls to 44x44 targets where direct manipulation remains allowed. `VideoEditorShell` now applies larger touch targets to shell and preview action controls, adds live screen-reader labels for interaction state, and adds reduced-motion-safe transition fallbacks. Verified with `npm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false` and `npm exec vitest run src/tools/video-editor/components/__tests__/PreviewPersistence.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/components/PreviewPanel/RemotionPreview.test.tsx`.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Yes. Preview overlay editing now follows the same interaction contract as the timeline: overlay transforms start from pointer events, claim shared `gestureOwner` as `preview`, and release that ownership when the interaction ends. Phone touch remains inspector-first because overlay transform handles and preview-side transform buttons are hidden there and touch pointer-down only selects and routes focus back to inspector state, while tablet touch and mouse/trackpad paths keep direct manipulation. `PreviewPanel` and `VideoEditorShell` also add live interaction-state labels, larger touch targets, and reduced-motion-safe transitions so preview controls stay accessible without stealing touches from the mode bar, inspector, or timeline.
